senses.definition: in a way that is given to drinking or affecting by drink; habitually inclined to drink alcohol
examples: He bibulously sipped the wine at every toast.
The tavern was bibulously welcoming to weary travelers.

senses.definition: the tendency to drink alcohol or to be absorbent; eagerness to drink or absorb
examples: His bibulousness was well known among the patrons.
Bibulousness often leads to extended evenings at the bar.

senses.definition: plural of bibulousness; instances of bibulousness
examples: Several bibulousnesses were noted after the festival.
The report cataloged their bibulousnesses across weeks.

senses.definition: a person who drinks; a drinker
examples: The bibulus walked home slowly after the party.
Locals teased the bibulus for his constant sipping.

senses.definition: having two calcareous projections or spurs
examples: The bicalcarate leaf had two small spurs near its base.
A bicalcarate structure was observed in the fossil.

senses.definition: having two sharp or pointed features; twin spurs or points
examples: The bicalvous seed resembles two small horns.
A bicalvous projection marked the crest.

senses.definition: having two chambers or houses, especially in a legislative body
examples: The country has a bicameral parliament consisting of two houses.
Many nations debate reforms to their bicameral system.

senses.definition: the practice or principle of having two chambers in a legislative body
examples: Bicameralism aims to provide checks and balances.
Scholars discuss the advantages of bicameralism.

senses.definition: plural of bicameralism
examples: Various bicameralisms exist, differing in the powers of each house.
The study compared several bicameralisms across nations.

senses.definition: a person who advocates or supports bicameralism
examples: The bicameralist argued for a stronger upper house.
As a bicameralist, she believed two chambers improve deliberation.

senses.definition: pertaining to or supporting bicameralism
examples: The bicameralist party pushed for reform.
Her bicameralist views shaped the draft constitution.

senses.definition: plural of bicameralist
examples: The bicameralists debated the boundaries of power.
Historic debates feature many bicameralists.

senses.definition: a person who favors or supports two legislative chambers
examples: As a bicameralist, he preferred a two-house legislature.
The side of bicameralist argued for stronger upper houses.

senses.definition: having two heads or heads at two locations
examples: A rare bicapitate seed emerged from the fruit.
The sculpture showed a bicapitate creature.

senses.definition: having two capsules
examples: The plant features a bicapsular fruit.
Researchers noted a bicapsular structure in the sample.

senses.definition: short for bicarbonate; a chemical compound (sodium bicarbonate) often used in baking or medicine
examples: Add a pinch of bicarb to react with the acid.
Bicarb is commonly used as an antacid.

senses.definition: a compound containing two carbon atoms bonded to boron or other elements; less common
examples: The lab prepared a sample of the bicarbide compound.
Bicarbonide forms were discussed in the inorganic course.

senses.definition: a chemical compound NaHCO3 used in baking, medicine, and chemistry
examples: Baking soda is the same as bicarbonate of soda.
A bicarbonate solution can neutralize acids.

senses.definition: plural of bicarbonate
examples: The pantry holds several bicarbonates for chemistry experiments.
Different brands of bicarbonates are sold in stores.

senses.definition: informal short for bicarbonates; various bicarbonate salts
examples: Keep the bicarbs in a cool, dry place.
The recipe calls for bicarbs to be measured precisely.

senses.definition: having been infused or saturated with bicarbonate or a bicarbonide compound
examples: The solution was bicarbureted to adjust pH.
A bicarbureted mixture reacted differently.

senses.definition: infused with bicarbonate; same as bicarbureted (British spelling variant)
examples: The bicarburetted liquid had a mild effervescence.
Bicarburetted waters are sometimes used in experiments.

senses.definition: having two karat or karats? (likely misprint; intended to mean two karinate groups) In absence of standard usage, treat as rare/obsolete
examples: A bicarinate ring appeared in the historical specimen.

senses.definition: having two carpels or seed-containing ovaries in a flower
examples: The plant is bicarpellary, with two distinct ovaries.
Botanists noted the bicarpellary structure in the specimen.

senses.definition: having two carpels or ovaries in a flower; two-chambered fruit
examples: Bicarpellate fruit develops from two carpels.
The flower shows a bicarpellate arrangement.

senses.definition: having two tails or tail-like projections
examples: The butterfly displayed a bicaudal pattern on its hindwings.
In the specimen, bicaudal features were evident.
